title: Tell Hebua I (Ancient Tjaru)
description: The starting point of the "Way of Horus" and headquarters of the Egyptian army. Massive Pharaonic fortifications. Qantara East, New Kingdom.

title: Tell Hebua II
description: Extension of the Tjaru complex with storage magazines and administrative buildings. Qantara East, New Kingdom/Hyksos.

title: Tell el-Borg
description: Site of the "Forts of the Lion" defending the frontier. Evidence of moats and barracks. Northwest Sinai, New Kingdom.

title: Tell Abu Seify (Sile)
description: Site of the Roman fortress of Sile (Silla) and Temple of Zeus. Qantara East, Roman Period.

title: Tell el-Kedwa
description: A Saite period fortress with massive mudbrick walls, destroyed by Persian invasion. Qantara East, 26th Dynasty.

title: Tell el-Ghaba
description: Unfortified settlement on the ancient lagoon edge. Northwest Sinai, 10th-7th Century BC.

title: Tell el-Farama (Pelusium)
description: The "Key to Egypt". Features a Late Roman fortress, theater, baths, and the Farama church. Baluarte, Greco-Roman/Byzantine.

title: Tell el-Makhzan
description: Part of the Pelusium greater area, containing the "Eastern Church" basilica and kilns. Baluarte, Byzantine.

title: Tell el-Kana'is
description: The "Hill of Churches", containing remains of Byzantine ecclesiastical structures. Baluarte area.

title: Tell el-Fadda
description: Site north of Pelusium with remains from the Late Period to Ptolemaic eras. Baluarte area.

title: Tell el-Herr (Migdol)
description: Identified as biblical Migdol. A sequence of fortresses from Persian to Roman times. Northwest Sinai.

title: Tell el-Felusiyat (Ostrakine)
description: Ruins of the Byzantine city Ostrakine with three basilicas and a fortress. Lake Bardawil coast.

title: Tell el-Ghurifa
description: Iron Age/Persian settlement and customs station near Ostrakine. Lake Bardawil.

title: Ras Qasroun (Mount Casius)
description: Sand dune promontory associated with the Temple of Zeus Casius. Lake Bardawil.

title: Tell el-Qawadis
description: Site east of Lake Bardawil with a temple (possibly Aphrodite) and defensive structure.

title: Al-Zaraniq (Floumy)
description: Ancient port area and waystation with pottery workshops. West of Arish.

title: Citadel of Al-Arish
description: Ottoman fortress built by Sultan Qansuh al-Ghouri, later used by Napoleon. Al-Arish City.

title: Tell el-Sheikh Zuweid
description: Hellenistic and Roman settlement famous for the Sheikh Zuweid Mosaic. Sheikh Zuweid.

title: Tell Rafah (Raphia)
description: Site of the ancient city Raphia and the famous battle of 217 BC. Rafah border.

title: Nakhl Fortress
description: Strategic Mamluk/Ottoman fortress on the Hajj route (Darb al-Hajj). Central Sinai (Nakhl).

title: Fort of Tina
description: Ottoman fort built to control coastal access near Pelusium. Baluarte area.

title: Tell el-Ahmar
description: Site containing Late Period and Roman remains near Hebua.

title: Tell el-Luli
description: Archaeological mound associated with the eastern delta defensive network. Qantara area.

title: Khirbet el-Duweidar
description: Middle Bronze Age settlement site. Northern Sinai littoral.

title: Tell el-Mufarriq
description: Site showing evidence of Roman/Byzantine occupation south of the coastal route.
